title: BC FLA Separation Agreement — Children & Parenting Sub-Schema
notes: All parenting provisions remain subject to the court's best-interests jurisdiction under FLA s. 37 and may be varied if circumstances change materially.

legal_name.description: Full legal name of the child as shown on birth certificate or adoption order.

sex_or_gender.description: Sex or gender identity of the child. Free text; optional.

province_of_birth.description: Canadian province or territory of birth, or country if born outside Canada.

parentage_type.description: Relationship basis. 'standing_in_place_of_parent' engages FLA s. 147 [VERIFY] regarding a person who is not a biological or adoptive parent but has demonstrated settled intention to treat the child as their own (see also FLA s. 146 [VERIFY]).

school_name.description: Current school attended by the child.

primary_residence_address.description: Address of the child's primary residence for enrollment and records purposes.

special_needs_or_medical.description: Any diagnosed special needs, chronic medical conditions, or ongoing treatment requirements. Optional; free text.

day.description: Day of the week for this block.

with_parent.description: Which parent has the child during this block.

holiday_name.description: Name of the holiday or special day (e.g., Christmas Day, Thanksgiving, Mother's Day).

allocated_to.description: Which parent has the child, or rotation pattern.

rotation_rule.description: Rotation details, e.g., 'Spouse 1 in even years, Spouse 2 in odd years.'

domain.description: Category of parental decision-making per FLA s. 41 [VERIFY].

allocation.description: Who holds decision-making authority. 'consult_then_one_decides' requires meaningful consultation before the designated parent makes the final call.

deciding_parent_if_applicable.description: The parent with final authority when allocation is 'consult_then_one_decides' or sole. Omit for joint.

notes.description: Additional terms or qualifications for this domain.

allocation_method.description: How the break is divided, e.g., 'First three weeks with Spouse 1, remaining with Spouse 2, alternating annually.'

notification_deadline_days.description: Days before the break starts by which each parent must confirm plans.

children.description: All children of the relationship to whom this agreement applies.

parenting_time.description: Parenting time arrangements per FLA ss. 40–42 [VERIFY].

schedule_type.description: High-level characterization of the parenting time arrangement.

primary_parent_if_applicable.description: Parent with whom the child primarily resides, if schedule_type is 'primary_with_one_parent'.

holiday_schedule.description: Named holidays and special days with allocation and rotation rules.

break_allocations.description: Summer, winter, and spring break allocations.

transition_location.description: Location for exchanges, e.g., school, neutral public place, or a parent's residence.

time_of_day_rules.description: Any constraints on transition timing, e.g., 'No transitions after 8:00 PM on school nights.'

transitions.description: Rules governing physical exchanges of the child between parents.

enabled.description: Whether the other parent must be offered care before a third-party caregiver is used.

threshold_hours.description: Minimum number of consecutive hours of absence that triggers the obligation.

right_of_first_refusal.description: Right of first refusal for childcare during the other parent's scheduled time.

parental_responsibilities.description: Allocation of parental responsibilities (decision-making) per FLA s. 41 [VERIFY].

domains.description: Each decision-making domain with its allocation.

dispute_resolution_for_deadlock.description: Process when jointly deciding parents cannot agree, e.g., mediation, parenting coordinator, arbitration.

communication.description: Communication protocols between parents and with children.

parent_to_parent_channel.description: Required communication platform, e.g., 'OurFamilyWizard', 'email only', or named app.

response_time_hours.description: Maximum hours within which a parent must respond to a non-emergency communication.

emergency_response_time_hours.description: Maximum hours for response to an emergency communication.

frequency.description: How often the non-residential parent may contact the child, e.g., 'once daily'.

mode.description: Permitted mode of contact, e.g., 'phone call, video call'.

time_window.description: Acceptable window for contact, e.g., '6:00 PM – 7:30 PM'.

parent_child_contact_during_other_time.description: Terms for a parent contacting the child during the other parent's parenting time.

no_disparagement.description: Whether the agreement includes a mutual prohibition on disparaging the other parent in the child's presence or hearing.

relocation_and_mobility.description: Relocation provisions per FLA Part 4, Division 6 (ss. 65–71) [VERIFY].

notice_period_days.description: Minimum days of written notice required before a proposed relocation. FLA s. 66 prescribes a minimum of 60 days [VERIFY].

relocation_definition.description: What constitutes a 'relocation' triggering notice obligations, e.g., move exceeding a specified distance or change of school district.

distance_threshold_km.description: Distance in kilometres beyond which a move is treated as a relocation.

dispute_resolution_before_move.description: Required dispute-resolution steps (mediation, arbitration, court application) before either parent may relocate with a child.

travel_and_passports.description: International and domestic travel provisions and passport custody.

international_travel_consent_process.description: Steps required to obtain the other parent's consent for international travel, including form of consent (written/notarized) and advance notice period.

advance_notice_days.description: Days of notice required before international travel with the child.

passport_held_by.description: Which parent retains physical custody of the child's passport.

passport_retrieval_process.description: How the non-holding parent obtains the passport for approved travel.

required.description: Whether the travelling parent must provide a detailed itinerary.

details.description: Categories of travel information that must be disclosed.

disclosure_deadline_days.description: Days before departure by which the itinerary must be provided.

itinerary_disclosure.description: Notification requirements for travel itinerary, accommodations, and return.

review_and_variation.description: Scheduled reviews and variation mechanism for parenting provisions.

trigger.description: Event or date triggering review, e.g., 'child turns 13', 'child enters high school', or a calendar date.

scope.description: What is reviewed, e.g., 'parenting time schedule', 'all parenting provisions'.

scheduled_reviews.description: Pre-agreed points at which the parties will review parenting arrangements.

material_change_clause.description: Text affirming either party may seek variation upon a material change in circumstances affecting the child's best interests, consistent with FLA s. 47 [VERIFY].

dispute_resolution_path.description: Ordered steps before court application, e.g., ['negotiation', 'mediation', 'arbitration', 'court_application'].
